core_mechanism: a single continuous-parameter geometric deformation of a translationally-uniform 1D chain of coupled sites, implemented by grading the nearest-neighbor coupling strengths exponentially with position (weights proportional to cosh(j*lambda)) so the discrete chain is effectively embedded in a curved (hyperbolic) space, with the uniform system recovered as the parameter goes to zero and spatial shifts generated by a boundary/corner operator
brief_reason: The abstract defines non-uniform Hamiltonians whose nearest-neighbor interaction strength is proportional to cosh(j*lambda) in the lattice index j, tied to hyperbolic 1+1 space, reducing to the uniform case as lambda->0 and translated by corner Hamiltonians, i.e. an exponential position-grading of couplings that curves an otherwise uniform chain.
